semantic.role: Defines a structured template for submitting feature requests to the ArchSpine project, standardizing the format and required information from contributors.
responsibilities: Collecting a clear problem statement that motivates the feature request
Gathering a detailed description of the proposed solution
Documenting alternative solutions the requester has considered
Providing space for additional context, screenshots, or supporting details
outOfScope: Bug reports or defect tracking (handled elsewhere)
Pull request templates or code contribution guidelines
Project governance, roadmap, or architectural decisions

English.purpose: This document provides a GitHub issue template for feature requests in the ArchSpine project. It ensures that every suggestion follows a consistent structure, making it easier for maintainers and community members to evaluate and discuss new ideas.
English.context_and_audience: The primary audience is anyone — users, contributors, or stakeholders — who wants to propose a new feature or enhancement to ArchSpine. The template guides them to clearly articulate the problem, their proposed solution, alternatives considered, and any additional context. It lives in the repository at `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.yml` and is automatically presented when opening a new issue of type 'Feature Request'.
key_takeaways: The template collects four structured fields: problem, solution, alternatives, and additional context.
The 'problem' and 'solution' fields are required; alternatives and context are optional.
Using this template helps maintainers quickly understand the value and completeness of a feature suggestion.
It enforces a problem-first approach, asking 'Is your feature request related to a problem?' before jumping to solutions.
